activities_projects.title: Meeting Dr. Dolittle
activities_projects.objective: Identify the unique traits of the main character
steps: Read the introduction of Dr. Dolittle
List three things that make him different from a normal doctor
Draw a small picture of Dr. Dolittle surrounded by his favorite animals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand character introduction and traits
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of character traits (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Animal Language Dictionary
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of animal communication in the story
steps: Find the part where Polynesia the parrot talks about animal language
Pick three animals from the story (e.g., dog, duck, owl)
Write down the 'word' or sound they use and what it means in English according to the story
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students grasp the imaginative element of animal speech
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in matching animal sounds to their meanings (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Polynesia’s Wisdom
activities_projects.objective: Recognize the role of the parrot as a teacher
steps: Identify why Polynesia decided to teach Dr. Dolittle
Write 2-3 sentences on how she helped him become a better animal doctor
List two qualities of Polynesia (e.g., clever, helpful)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the relationship between characters
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of explanation regarding the parrot's role (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Humorous Conversations
activities_projects.objective: Identify wit and humor in the dialogue
steps: Find a funny conversation between the Doctor and an animal
Copy the funniest line into your notebook
Explain in one sentence why you find it funny
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize basic humor and wit in literature
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of humor and simple reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Animal Roll Call
activities_projects.objective: List and describe the animals in the Doctor's house
steps: Go through the chapter and find the names of all the animals living with the Doctor
Write their names (e.g., Jip, Dab-Dab, Too-Too)
Write what kind of animal each one is next to their name
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice scanning the text for specific information
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness and accuracy of the animal list (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Doctor’s New Sign
activities_projects.objective: Practice creative thinking and simple design
steps: The Doctor stops being a 'people doctor' to become an 'animal doctor'
Design a new sign for his clinic door
Make sure to include a catchy slogan about animals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students apply the story's transition to a creative task
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and relevance to the story (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary: Animal Sounds
activities_projects.objective: Learn specific verbs for animal noises
steps: Find verbs in the text used for animal sounds (e.g., bark, quack, hoot)
Match 5 animals with their correct sounds in a table
Use one of these verbs in a sentence about your own pet or a local animal
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students expand their vocabulary of onomatopoeic words
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct matching and sentence structure (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sequence of Events
activities_projects.objective: Understand the order of the story
steps: Number these events 1-4: The Doctor learns animal language, Polynesia talks to the Doctor, People stop coming to the clinic, Animals start coming for treatment
Write the sentences in the correct order in your notebook

activities_projects.title: Acting Like an Animal
activities_projects.objective: Use body language to communicate without words
steps: Pick an animal from the story
Act out how that animal would 'ask' Dr. Dolittle for food without speaking
Have a classmate guess which animal you are and what you want
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students explore non-verbal communication mentioned in the text
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of expression (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: My Animal Friend
activities_projects.objective: Relate the story to personal experience
steps: Imagine you could talk to one animal for a day
Which animal would you choose and what is the first question you would ask?
Write 3-4 sentences about your conversation
